title: Mock Test 04 — Surface Mining & Mine Machinery

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: MINER : ORE :: FARMER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'worker : product extracted/produced'.

**Working.** A miner extracts ore; a farmer produces a crop. A tractor and field are tools/locations, not products.

**Answer.** crop.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The probability of getting a head in a single toss of a fair coin is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Probability = favourable / total equally likely outcomes.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{2}=0.5$.

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The least common multiple (LCM) of $6$ and $8$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** LCM is the product of all prime factors at highest powers.

**Working.** $6=2\cdot 3$, $8=2^3$; LCM $=2^3\cdot 3=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On the clock face shown, the time is $3{:}00$. Compute the smaller angle between the hour and minute hands (degrees).

figure.caption: Clock at 3:00
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='85' y2='28' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='133' y2='80' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='55' y='160' font-size='8'>time = 3:00</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hour mark is $30^\circ$ apart.

**Working.**
$$3\text{ hours}\times30^\circ=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the best connective: '____ the equipment was old, it functioned reliably.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A contrast between two clauses is introduced by a concessive conjunction.

**Working.** Old equipment working reliably is unexpected, so a contrast word ('Although') is needed; 'Because' would imply causation.

**Answer.** Although.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many rectangles (of all sizes) are there in a strip of three unit cells in a row (shown)?

figure.caption: A horizontal strip divided into three equal unit cells.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='20' width='150' height='40' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='65' y1='20' x2='65' y2='60' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='115' y1='20' x2='115' y2='60' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count rectangles spanning $1$, $2$ or $3$ cells.

**Working.** Single cells $=3$, pairs $=2$, the full strip $=1$; total $=3+2+1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article sold for $480$ incurs a loss of $20\%$. Its cost price is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $20\%$ loss means SP $=0.8\times$ CP.

**Working.**
$$\text{CP}=\frac{480}{0.8}=600$$

**Answer.** $600$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in both blanks: 'Because the data were ____, the analysts could draw only ____ conclusions.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Because' links a cause (quality of data) to an effect (strength of conclusions).

**Working.** Poor ('incomplete') data justify only cautious ('tentative') conclusions; the other pairs imply strong data with strong conclusions, contradicting the limitation.

**Answer.** incomplete ... tentative.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A man walks $3$ km north and then $4$ km east. His straight-line distance from the starting point is _____ km.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perpendicular legs form a right triangle; use Pythagoras.

**Working.** $\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=\sqrt{25}=5$.

**Answer.** $5$ km.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the area of the circle of radius $7$ shown (use $\pi=3.1416$, two decimals).

figure.caption: Circle of radius 7
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='90' cy='85' r='60' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='90' y1='85' x2='150' y2='85' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='105' y='80' font-size='8'>r=7</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Compared with the true dip, the apparent dip in any other vertical section is
options: always less than or equal to the true dip
questions.solution:
**Concept.** True dip is the maximum dip.

**Reasoning.** Since $\tan\delta_a=\tan\delta_t\sin\theta$ and $\sin\theta\le1$, the apparent dip cannot exceed the true dip; they are equal only when the section is perpendicular to strike (θ = 90°).

**Answer.** ≤ true dip.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Placer deposits of gold, cassiterite (tin) and diamond form by
options: mechanical concentration of heavy, chemically resistant minerals by flowing water or wind
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Density and durability drive placer formation.

**Reasoning.** Weathering releases heavy, hard, chemically stable minerals; running water (or wind) winnows the light material and concentrates the dense grains in stream beds, beaches and terraces.

**Answer.** Mechanical (placer) concentration.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If a fan's speed is increased by $10\%$, the quantity delivered increases by _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q\propto N$.

**Working.** A $10\%$ rise in $N$ gives a $10\%$ rise in $Q$.

**Answer.** $10\%$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock with thermal expansion coefficient $1\times10^{-5}\,/^\circ\text{C}$ undergoes a temperature rise of $50^\circ\text{C}$. The free thermal strain, expressed as a multiple of $10^{-4}$, is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Thermal strain $=\alpha\,\Delta T$.

**Working.**
$$\varepsilon=(1\times10^{-5})(50)=5\times10^{-4}.$$

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: At a truss joint, one vertical member and one horizontal member meet, and an external vertical load of $100\,\text{N}$ acts at the joint. The force in the vertical member is _____ N.

figure.caption: A joint with a vertical and a horizontal member and a 100 N downward external load.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='80' y1='90' x2='80' y2='20' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='80' y1='90' x2='150' y2='90' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='80' y1='90' x2='80' y2='130' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='80,130 74,118 86,118' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='60' y='145' font-size='11' fill='#ef4444'>100 N</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Vertical equilibrium at the joint: only the vertical member balances the vertical load.

**Working.** $F_{\text{vert}}=100\,\text{N}$ (the horizontal member carries no vertical component).

**Answer.** $100\,\text{N}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A pit is mined to 90 m depth in benches of 15 m height. The number of benches is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Number of benches $=\dfrac{\text{pit depth}}{\text{bench height}}$.

**Working.**
$$n=\frac{90}{15}=6$$

**Answer.** 6 benches.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A $3\times3$ matrix has eigenvalues $1,\,2,\,4$. Its determinant is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\det(A)=\prod \lambda_i$.

**Working.**
$$\det(A)=1\times 2\times 4=8$$

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Carbon dioxide is emitted at $0.03\,\text{m}^3$/s. To hold its concentration to $0.5\%$, the air required is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=\dfrac{q_{\text{gas}}}{C_{\text{allow}}}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\frac{0.03}{0.005}=6.$$

**Answer.** $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways, each of resistance $4\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$, are in parallel. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt4}+\dfrac{1}{\sqrt4}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=0.5+0.5=1\Rightarrow R_{eq}=1.$$

**Answer.** $1\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: An asset costing $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$ (life $5$ years) is depreciated by DDB. The first-year depreciation is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Two equal, incoherent noise sources each produce 90 dB at a point. Their combined level (adding 3 dB for a doubling of energy), in dB, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Doubling sound energy adds 3 dB.

**Working.**
$$L=90+10\log_{10}2=90+3=93\ \text{dB}$$

**Answer.** 93 dB.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: If the machine operates 9 h out of a 16-h scheduled shift, its overall use-of-time factor is ____ (four decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Use-of-time $=\dfrac{\text{operating}}{\text{scheduled}}=A\times U$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{9}{16}=0.5625\quad(=0.75\times0.75)$$

**Answer.** 0.5625.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The angle of draw in subsidence engineering is
options: the angle from the vertical between the panel edge and the limit of surface subsidence
the slope of the subsidence trough at its steepest point
the friction angle of the overburden
questions.solution:
**Concept.** It defines the lateral extent of the subsidence basin.

**Working.** Measured from vertical at the working edge to the trough limit on surface.

**Answer.** the angle from the vertical between the panel edge and the limit of surface subsidence.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Phased (pushback / cutback) mining within the ultimate pit is practised mainly to
options: defer waste removal and expose ore earlier, improving early cash flow and NPV
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Not all the waste in the final pit need be moved at once.

**Reasoning.** Dividing the pit into nested phases lets early phases target low-stripping, high-grade ore, deferring deeper waste. This brings revenue forward and reduces the present value of stripping cost, raising NPV.

**Answer.** Defer waste, expose ore early, improve NPV.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A blast pattern has 50 holes, each 10 m deep. The total drilled meterage, in m, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total metres $=$ holes $\times$ depth.

**Working.**
$$L=50\times10=500\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 500 m.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Over the life of mine, 50 Mm³ of overburden must be moved to extract 10 Mt of ore (as shown). Compute the overall (life-of-mine) stripping ratio (m³/t).

figure.caption: Life-of-mine volumes
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='70' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='120' y='70' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>OB 50 Mm^3</text><rect x='30' y='100' width='180' height='30' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='120' y='120' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>ore 10 Mt</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Overall SR = total overburden ÷ total ore.

**Working.**
$$SR = \frac{50}{10} = 5$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{5}$ m³/t.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A heading is driven with 3 blasting rounds per day, each pulling 1.5 m (as shown). Compute the daily drivage rate (m/day).

figure.caption: Heading advance
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,90 30,40 60,30 90,40 90,90' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='90' y1='65' x2='190' y2='65' stroke='#475569' stroke-dasharray='4 3' marker-end='url(#da)'/><defs><marker id='da' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='44' y='110' font-size='8'>3 rounds/day, pull 1.5 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Drivage rate = rounds per day × pull per round.

**Working.**
$$R = 3 \times 1.5 = 4.5$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{4.5}$ m/day.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Garland (catch) drains around the rim of an opencast pit are intended to
options: supply water to the haul road for dust suppression
intercept surface runoff and divert it away from the pit
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Keeping surface water out reduces pumping load and slope saturation.

**Reasoning.** Garland drains are perimeter channels that catch catchment runoff before it can enter the pit, diverting it to settling ponds, thereby protecting slopes and cutting dewatering cost.

**Answer.** Intercept and divert surface runoff away from the pit.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A specimen of length $500\,\text{mm}$ shortens by $0.5\,\text{mm}$ under load. The axial strain is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\varepsilon=\dfrac{\Delta L}{L}$.

**Working.**
$$\varepsilon=\frac{0.5}{500}=0.001.$$

**Answer.** $0.001$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The critical width of extraction is the width at which
options: the maximum possible subsidence is first achieved at a surface point
the angle of draw becomes zero
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Below critical width, subsidence is sub-maximal (subcritical).

**Working.** At critical width the centre of the basin first reaches $S_{max}$.

**Answer.** the maximum possible subsidence is first achieved.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The Schmidt hammer rebound number is most directly correlated with
options: rock surface hardness and uniaxial compressive strength
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rebound reflects surface stiffness/hardness.

**Working.** Empirical charts relate rebound (with density) to UCS.

**Answer.** rock surface hardness and UCS.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The 8-hour TLV-TWA for nitrogen dioxide (NO₂) is commonly taken as ____ ppm.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** NO₂ is toxic at low concentration.

**Reasoning.** Its 8-hour TLV-TWA is about 3 ppm (with a short-term limit around 5 ppm), reflecting its strong pulmonary irritancy.

**Answer.** 3 ppm.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: A total station integrates an electronic theodolite with an EDM. In a single pointing it can directly measure:
options: horizontal angle, vertical angle and slope distance

figure.caption: Total station measurement
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='80' y1='90' x2='55' y2='150' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='90' x2='105' y2='150' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='66' y='60' width='28' height='30' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='94' y1='75' x2='170' y2='50' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><rect x='168' y='40' width='12' height='24' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='20' y='28' font-size='8'>theodolite + EDM to prism</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A total station combines angular and distance measurement electronically.

**Reasoning.** In one observation to a prism it records the horizontal angle, the vertical (zenith) angle and the slope distance, from which coordinates and reduced levels are computed on board.

**Answer.** Horizontal angle, vertical angle and slope distance.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A walking dragline has a 40 m³ bucket and works at a fill factor of 0.85. The loose volume per cycle, in m³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Loose load $=$ bucket capacity $\times$ fill factor.

**Working.**
$$V=40\times0.85=34\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 34 m³ (loose).

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: In a soft rock the penetration rate is 4 m/min, while in a hard rock it is 2 m/min. The ratio of soft-to-hard penetration rate is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ratio of the two rates.

**Working.**
$$\frac{4}{2}=2$$

**Answer.** 2 (soft rock drills twice as fast).

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Local attraction in compass surveying is detected when
options: the difference between the fore and back bearings of a line is not exactly 180°
the level bubble is off centre
the offset exceeds the tape length
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Magnetic disturbance deflects the needle.

**Reasoning.** Nearby iron/magnetic material pulls the needle, so the observed fore and back bearings of a line differ by something other than 180°; stations free of attraction (180° difference) are used to correct the rest.

**Answer.** FB and BB differ by ≠ 180°.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries material of bulk density 1.5 t/m³ at a belt speed of 2 m/s with a load cross-sectional area of 0.1 m² (as shown). Compute the throughput (t/h).

figure.caption: Belt conveyor
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 250 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='18' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='210' cy='60' r='18' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='42' x2='210' y2='42' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='78' x2='210' y2='78' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M70 42 q20 -14 40 0' fill='#a16207'/><text x='52' y='100' font-size='8'>A=0.1 m^2, v=2 m/s, rho=1.5 t/m^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Throughput $Q = 3600 \, A \, v \, \rho$.

**Working.**
$$Q = 3600 \times 0.1 \times 2 \times 1.5 = 1080$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{1080}$ t/h.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem:
With $\sigma_v=13.5\,	ext{MPa}$ and $
u=0.2$ (uniaxial-strain $K_0=
u/(1-
u)$), the horizontal in-situ stress is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_h=K_0\sigma_v$, $K_0=\dfrac{\nu}{1-\nu}$.

**Working.**
$$K_0=\frac{0.2}{0.8}=0.25,\quad \sigma_h=0.25\times 13.5=3.38.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 3.38\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Effective Rate
questions.stem: When a nominal rate is compounded more than once per year, the effective annual rate is
options: always exactly double the nominal rate
questions.solution:
**Concept.** More frequent compounding earns interest on interest sooner.

**Working.** Hence $i_{eff}>r$ for $m>1$.

**Answer.** greater than the nominal rate.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock has cohesion $c=5\,\text{MPa}$ and friction angle $\phi=30^\circ$. The shear strength on a plane carrying normal stress $\sigma_n=20\,\text{MPa}$ is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.

figure.caption: Mohr circle with the Mohr-Coulomb failure envelope (c = 5 MPa, phi = 30 deg).

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau=c+\sigma_n\tan\phi$.

**Working.**
$$\tau=5+20\tan 30^\circ=5+11.55=16.55.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 16.55\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Two components of reliability $0.9$ and $0.8$ operate in parallel (redundant). The system reliability is _____ .

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The outward flux of $\mathbf{F}=x\,\hat{i}+y\,\hat{j}+z\,\hat{k}$ through the surface of the unit cube $[0,1]^{3}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** By the divergence theorem, flux $=\iiint_V(\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F})\,dV$.

**Working.** $\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=3$ and the cube has volume $1$:
$$\text{flux}=3\times 1=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Methane (gas) drainage — pre-draining the seam through boreholes before/while mining — is practised primarily to
options: increase the calorific value of the coal
reduce the methane emission into the working airways, lowering explosion risk and ventilation demand
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Capturing gas at source eases ventilation.

**Reasoning.** Draining methane through boreholes (and often utilising it) cuts the volume entering the workings, so less air is needed to dilute it and the explosion hazard falls — essential in highly gassy seams.

**Answer.** Reduce methane emission into the workings.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: For the bench shown (height $H=12$ m, burden $B=4$ m, spacing $S=5$ m), the in-situ volume of rock broken per blasthole, in $\mathrm{m^3}$, is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume per hole $=B\times S\times H$.

**Working.**
$$V=4\times5\times12=240\ \mathrm{m^3}$$

**Answer.** $240\ \mathrm{m^3}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A single observation has a standard deviation of 10 mm. The standard deviation of the mean of 4 independent observations, in mm, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_{\bar{x}}=\dfrac{\sigma}{\sqrt{n}}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_{\bar{x}}=\frac{10}{\sqrt{4}}=\frac{10}{2}=5\ \text{mm}$$

**Answer.** 5 mm.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: For the bench shown (height $H=10$ m, burden $B=3.5$ m), the stiffness ratio $H/B$ is _____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stiffness ratio $=\dfrac{H}{B}$; values of 3–4 give good fragmentation, below 2 risk poor breakage.

**Working.**
$$\frac{H}{B}=\frac{10}{3.5}=2.86$$

**Answer.** $2.86$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A 'syngenetic' ore deposit is one that
options: formed at the same time as the enclosing host rock
was introduced into pre-existing rock at a later time
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Timing relative to host rock classifies deposits.

**Reasoning.** Syngenetic deposits (e.g. BIF, magmatic segregations) form together with the host; epigenetic deposits (e.g. fissure veins) are emplaced later into existing rock.

**Answer.** Formed with the host rock.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: In open-pit terminology, the bench height is
options: the vertical height of a single working level
the horizontal width of a haul road
the depth of a blast hole
the angle of the pit wall
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A bench is one mining level.

**Working.** Its vertical dimension is the bench height.

**Answer.** the vertical height of a single working level.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The 'digging depth' of a dragline is
options: the maximum depth below the tub level it can excavate
the height of the boom point
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A dragline casts its bucket out and drags it back, excavating below its standing level.

**Reasoning.** Digging depth is the greatest depth below the tub that the dragged bucket can reach; it limits the overburden thickness a dragline can strip in one pass.

**Answer.** Maximum depth below the tub it can excavate.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Drawpoint spacing in a caving mine must be chosen so that
options: draw zones do not interact at all
the draw columns interact to give uniform draw, minimising ore loss and waste dilution
the spacing equals the shaft diameter
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Draw geometry controls recovery and dilution.

**Reasoning.** Spacing matched to fragmentation lets adjacent draw ellipsoids overlap (interactive draw), giving a near-uniform descent of the ore-waste interface; too-wide spacing leaves pillars of ore and lets waste finger down early.

**Answer.** So draw columns interact for uniform draw.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: If the muckpile shown is too coarse, the most direct design change to produce finer fragmentation is to:
options: increase the powder factor (tighter pattern / more charge)

figure.caption: Muckpile fragments
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#a16207' stroke='#92400e'><polygon points='30,90 55,80 70,100 45,110'/><polygon points='80,85 110,75 120,100 90,108'/><polygon points='130,90 150,82 162,102 138,110'/><polygon points='170,88 195,80 200,104 175,108'/></g><text x='40' y='125' font-size='8'>muckpile fragment sizes</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fragmentation gets finer as energy per unit volume (powder factor) rises.

**Reasoning.** A tighter pattern or higher charge raises the powder factor, putting more energy into each cubic metre of rock and reducing mean fragment size. Increasing burden/spacing would coarsen the muck.

**Answer.** Increase the powder factor (tighter pattern / more charge).

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The dumper must develop a rimpull equal to the total resistance of 98.1 kN while climbing at 10 km/h. The power required at the wheels, in kW, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Power $=$ force $\times$ speed (consistent units).

**Working.**
$$v=\frac{10}{3.6}=2.778\ \text{m/s}$$
$$P=98100\times2.778=272{,}500\ \text{W}=272.5\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** 272.5 kW.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Topsoil is stripped and stockpiled separately before mining so that
options: it can be sold as construction aggregate
it can be respread during reclamation to support revegetation
it reduces the swell factor of the overburden
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Topsoil carries the nutrients, seeds and microbes needed for plant growth.

**Reasoning.** Preserving it separately allows it to be replaced over the regraded spoil during reclamation, enabling successful revegetation and land restoration.

**Answer.** Respread during reclamation to support revegetation.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: The critical path of a project runs through three activities in series with durations $A=4$, $B=6$ and $C=5$ days (figure). Compute the project duration (days).

figure.caption: Series activity network
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><circle cx='30' cy='70' r='14'/><circle cx='110' cy='70' r='14'/><circle cx='190' cy='70' r='14'/></g><line x1='44' y1='70' x2='96' y2='70' stroke='#475569' marker-end='url(#a)'/><line x1='124' y1='70' x2='176' y2='70' stroke='#475569' marker-end='url(#a)'/><defs><marker id='a' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='56' y='60' font-size='8'>A=4</text><text x='136' y='60' font-size='8'>B=6</text><text x='92' y='112' font-size='8'>then C=5 from last node</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Project duration equals the sum of durations along the critical (longest) path.

**Working.**
$$T=4+6+5=15\ \text{days}$$

**Answer.** $15$ days.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: An overwind/overspeed protection device (e.g. a Lilly controller) on a winder
options: prevents the conveyance from being wound past its safe limit or exceeding safe speed
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Winding safety devices guard against the most dangerous winder faults.

**Reasoning.** Overwind (winding the conveyance into the headgear) and overspeed are catastrophic; the controller monitors position/speed and trips the brakes if safe limits are approached.

**Answer.** Prevents overwind/overspeed.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A machine is scheduled for 16 h but is down for repair for 4 h. Its mechanical availability is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Availability $=\dfrac{\text{scheduled}-\text{downtime}}{\text{scheduled}}$.

**Working.**
$$A=\frac{16-4}{16}=\frac{12}{16}=0.75$$

**Answer.** 0.75.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock has $E=50\,\text{GPa}$ and UCS $=100\,\text{MPa}$. Its modulus ratio $E/\text{UCS}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Modulus ratio $=\dfrac{E}{\text{UCS}}$ in consistent units.

**Working.**
$$\frac{50000\,\text{MPa}}{100\,\text{MPa}}=500.$$

**Answer.** $500$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following vector fields are solenoidal (divergence-free)?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Solenoidal means $\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=0$.

**Working.**
- $(y,-x)$: $0+0=0$ — TRUE.
- $(x,y)$: $1+1=2\neq0$ — FALSE.
- $(z,x,y)$: $0+0+0=0$ — TRUE.
- $(2x,-2y)$: $2-2=0$ — TRUE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (c) and (d).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A Mohr circle that reduces to a single point (zero radius) represents
options: a state of equal principal stresses (hydrostatic/isotropic)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Zero radius means no shear on any plane and equal normal stresses.

**Working.** This is an isotropic (hydrostatic) stress state, $\sigma_1=\sigma_2$.

**Answer.** a state of equal principal stresses.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Annuity Future Worth
questions.stem: Equal year-end deposits of $\textsf{Rs}\,10{,}000$ are made for $5$ years at $10\%$. The accumulated future worth is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which statements about $f(x)=|x|$ are correct?
options: It has a global minimum at $x=0$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continuity vs differentiability of the absolute-value function.

**Working.**
- Continuous at $0$ — TRUE.
- Differentiable at $0$ — FALSE (left slope $-1$, right slope $+1$).
- Global minimum at $0$ (value $0$) — TRUE.
- $f'(0)$ does not exist, so not $0$ — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a) and (c).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Using $\sigma_1=N\sigma_3+\sigma_c$ with $N=\tan^2(45^\circ+\phi/2)$, for $\phi=30^\circ$, $\sigma_c=30\,\text{MPa}$ and $\sigma_3=10\,\text{MPa}$, the axial stress at failure $\sigma_1$ is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $N=\tan^2(45^\circ+\phi/2)$ and $\sigma_1=N\sigma_3+\sigma_c$.

**Working.**
$$N=\tan^2 60^\circ=3,\quad \sigma_1=3(10)+30=60.$$

**Answer.** $60\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A point noise source (see figure) produces a sound level of 90 dB at a distance of 1 m. Assuming spherical spreading (inverse-square attenuation, $L_2=L_1-20\log_{10}(r_2/r_1)$), the level at 10 m, in dB, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='80' r='10' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='40' y='112' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>source</text><circle cx='40' cy='80' r='22' fill='none' stroke='#f59e0b'/><circle cx='40' cy='80' r='34' fill='none' stroke='#f59e0b'/><circle cx='220' cy='80' r='6' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='220' y='112' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>receiver</text><line x1='52' y1='80' x2='212' y2='80' stroke='#475569' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='110' y='72' font-size='9'>r2 = 10 m</text><text x='8' y='52' font-size='8'>90 dB @ 1 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Noise spreading from a point source

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a point source, level drops 20 dB per decade of distance.

**Working.**
$$L_2=90-20\log_{10}\!\left(\frac{10}{1}\right)=90-20\times1=70\ \text{dB}$$

**Answer.** $70$ dB.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock specimen shows an axial stress of $50\,\text{MPa}$ at an axial strain of $0.001$. Its Young's modulus is _____ GPa.

figure.caption: A linear stress-strain plot with stress on the vertical axis and strain on the horizontal axis.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='115' x2='160' y2='115' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='25' y1='115' x2='25' y2='15' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='25' y1='115' x2='140' y2='30' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><text x='130' y='130' font-size='10' fill='#334155'>strain</text><text x='2' y='30' font-size='10' fill='#334155'>stress</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $E=\dfrac{\sigma}{\varepsilon}$.

**Working.**
$$E=\frac{50}{0.001}=50000\,\text{MPa}=50\,\text{GPa}.$$

**Answer.** $50\,\text{GPa}$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Taking stemming length = 0.7 × burden, the stemming for a 4.5 m burden, in m, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $T=0.7\,B$.

**Working.**
$$T=0.7\times4.5=3.15\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 3.15 m.
